rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF PATRICIA ANN FIEBIG
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #7, Report
rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) It does not appear that the amendment filed on 11/14/23 has been properly verified. A copy of the amendment is attached to the 2nd amendment filed on 3/26/24, but the verification to the 2nd amendment does not address the contents of the 1st amendment. 2) Carry values were not provided in the Property on Hand schedules attached to the 1st and 2nd amendments. 3) It appears that the fair market values of bonds have been used, rather than the carry values, in the accounting. 4) Prior attorney (Kato, Feder, Suzuki) was paid $5,435 on 7/13/21. It does not appear that there was a court order, and Petitioner is unable to provide any invoices. 5) The current bond is $165,000, and Petitioner has calculated the total bond needed as $168,214.97; however, he does not request an increase in bond as he alleges that $3,648.87 in tangible personal property has no resale value. It is noted, however, that Petitioner's estimated fair market value of the personal property is still listed as $3,648.87. 6) Notice of Hearing has not been given. 7) Proposed order has not been submitted. Remote appearances may be made. Please review Local Rule 14.1. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 160 225 4765 Password: 514879 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-254-5252, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above.

rulings.Case Title: CONSERVATORSHIP OF MATTHEW MCMILLAN
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #4, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The order approving the third account permitted the conservator to transfer $1,201.08 (as of December 31, 2021), plus accrued interest, from the set-aside account to pay conservator and attorney compensation. That order also permitted fees to be paid from the SNT to the extent funds in this estate were insufficient. Why are there fees still due? (2) Why did $500 need to be set aside? Was the conservatee at risk of not receiving government benefits? (3) The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth in an accounting. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (It would be helpful if the conservatorship petition also addressed the disbursements and confirmed that the conservator reviews all receipts before reimbursing individuals since most reimbursements do not indicate who the original payees were.)

rulings.Case Title: IN RE: MATTHEW MCMILLAN SPECIAL NEEDS TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting #3, Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) The computer printout of the financial statement is not clear. (2) The financial statement also ends on 1/31/24, not 12/31/23, so it does not verify the assets on hand as of 12/31/23. For example, the faint copy of the statement does not show 293 shares of a US Treasury Note and all of the FMVs differ between the accounting and the statement. (3) Some of the carry values also do not agree between the accounting and the statement, such as the shares of Crown Castle, Entergy, Kinder Morgan, and Williams Companies. (4) How were the bond fees calculated? (5) Why are there conservatee and attorney fees still due when the trust estate appears to have sufficient funds to pay them? How were the amounts due determined? At the end of the third accounting, there was no liabilities schedule showing unpaid fees from the second account. (6) The date of any order authorizing fees must be set forth in an accounting. [CRC 7.575(e)(6)(C).] (7) A new bed, chest of drawers, and a recliner purchased during this accounting period are not assets on hand at the end of the accounting period? (8) Some of the meals appear to be quite high Please provide courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ELIZABETH WILLIAMS
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Accounting, Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/24/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) Although the amended petition was "filed" (uploaded) on 5/3/24, it was not e-filed until 5/6/24, which was not at least 15 days before this hearing. [LRSF 14.14 A.] (NB: Issues posted for the 4/10/24 hearing clearly directed that notice be given AFTER the amendment was e-filed.) (2) The accounting schedules still do not comport with Probate Code Sections 1060, et seq. Accounting entries must include the DATE of each distinct transaction and not lump sum amounts. (3) The distribution from Freddie A. Williams, Sr.'s estate was $3,611.28, not $3,586.31. (4) Why was there a receipt of $28,590.74 from the post-deceased son's estate? (5) Granddaughter Felicia Williams is now deceased (on 1/9/24). Who is her successor-in-interest? If no one has been appointed for her estate, the hearing might need to be continued further so that notice to that personal representative can be given (or waived by that person). (6) Distribution cannot be to an "estate;" it must be to the personal representative (a specific person). (7) The list of reimbursable expenses, besides being in less than 12-point type [CRC 2.104], needs further clarification. The truncated copies of Renard Lee Williams's Bank of America statements with most of the amounts redacted do not appear to correspond to or support anything. (8) Administrator does not address what actions he took under his IAEA authority. [CRC 7.250.] (9) Attorney costs have not been set forth in detail. (10) Notice of Hearing must have attached to it the procedures for how to make a remote appearance by Zoom and CourtCall, as well as information about remote public access, as set out at https://sf.courts.ca.gov/divisions/probate-court. [LRSF 14.1 C.6.] Instructions should not be in item 1 of the notice, which should just identify the petitioner, his representative capacity, and the pleading to be heard. Deadline is 6/14/24 for providing the assigned examiner with courtesy copies of any responsive papers. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B. (The administrator, not an executor, was appointed on 1/3/22 but did not claim Letters until 6/1/22. The creditor claims period expired on 10/1/22, not 3/12/23. The register of actions does not show a request for special notice filed by anyone. This estate paid personal property taxes? It does not appear that all of the funds are held in blocked accounts; only the Wells Fargo Bank statement clearly indicates that it is a blocked account. The attorney never completed the Statement About the Bond on either I&A.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE GERTHA R. CURTIS REVOCABLE TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Appoint Lise Mccarthy, Private Professional Fiduciary, As Successor Trustee Of The Gertha R. Curtis Revocable Trust, Executed December 9, 2021
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 7/3/24. (No earlier availability.) Issue(s) necessitating continuance: (1) No proposed order has been lodged yet or received by the assigned examiner. [LRSF 14.7 A. and 14.59 E.] (2) The petition (as reviewed online, as no courtesy copy was provided) indicates that grandson (?) Michael Duane Curtis is deceased (10/13/22), but the trust identifies him as the trustorâs son. (3) Notice was given to Michael Jason Curtis, son, and to Michael Duane Curtis, grandson, both apparently still living. (4) The petition and Notice of Hearing indicate that the trust was "executed December 9, 2021," but the copy of the fully executed trust attached to the petition shows that it was executed on 10/6/22. (5) Lise McCarthy's consent does not appear to have been filed separately or attached to the petition. (6) Paragraph 2 of the petition mentions that waivers of conflict will be filed prior to this hearing, but no waivers appear to have been filed. Please provide a proposed order and courtesy copies of any papers filed to address these issues to the assigned examiner by 6/24/24. Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.
